```{r}
    x = "Month",
    y = "Leaf dry matter content",
    title = "Distribution of LDMC by Month and Age Class"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

```

### IQR-flagging LDMC

```{r}
#| label: LDMC  iqr flagging 
#| message: false

# same logic as above

df_for_flagging_ldmc <- data |>     
  filter(proj_2025 == "Traits") |>      
  filter(!is.na(ldmc))


trait_var_ldmc <- "ldmc"


group_vars_ldmc <- c("age_class", "month", "plant_nr")


data_flagged_ldmc <- df_for_flagging_ldmc |>  
  filter(!is.na(.data[[trait_var_ldmc]])) |>  
  group_by(across(all_of(group_vars_ldmc))) |>  
  mutate(
    q1  = quantile(.data[[trait_var_ldmc]], 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3  = quantile(.data[[trait_var_ldmc]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr = q3 - q1,
    lo  = q1 - 1.5 * iqr,
    hi  = q3 + 1.5 * iqr,
    flag_iqr = .data[[trait_var_ldmc]] < lo | 
               .data[[trait_var_ldmc]] > hi
  ) |> 
  ungroup()


outlier_review_ldmc <- data_flagged_ldmc |> 
  filter(flag_iqr) |> 
  dplyr::select(all_of(group_vars_ldmc),
                envelopeID,
                leaf_nr,
                ldmc,
                lo, hi)


# potting 
if (all(c("month", "age_class") %in% group_vars_ldmc)) {
  
  ggplot(data_flagged_ldmc,
          aes(x = factor(plant_nr),
             y = .data[[trait_var_ldmc]])) +
    
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
    
    geom_point(
      aes(colour = factor(plant_nr)),
      alpha = 0.35,
      position = position_jitter(width = 0.15)
    ) +
    
    geom_point(
      data = data_flagged_ldmc |> filter(flag_iqr),
      position = position_jitter(width = 0.15),
      colour = "red",
      size = 2
    ) +
    
    facet_grid(age_class ~ month) +
    
    labs(
      x = "Faceted by month, plant nr on x-axis",
      y = "LDMC",
      title = "Leaf Dry Matter Content grouped by Month and Age Class (with IQR flags)"
    ) +
    
    theme_bw()
}


# 32 OUTLIERS FLAGGED FOR LDMC. 
# SOME VALUES ARE VERY HIGH, NOT SURE WHY EVEN AFTER CROSS CHECKING. 
# 5 envelopes to be re-checked:
#DKD1356, nb_nov_5:  wet mass entered correctly. dry mass entred correctly. bulk_nr mistake. only 2 leaves at wet mass, but dry mass bulk nr column had value 4. corrected in code further up. this solved the issue. no longer outlier
#DKG1511, nb_nov_6. wet mass, dry mass, bulk nr correct. redried and weighed, corrected in code further up. solved the issue. no longer outlier. 
#DLW1760, bui_nov_5: wet mass data entry mistake. does not match envelope. entered as 0.019 while envelope says 0.01190. still outlier. REDRY AND WEIGH.  still outlier, keep in. 
#DMW4615, mat_nov_6.  wet mass, dry mass, bulk nr correct. REDRY AND WEIGH. still outlier, consider removing. 
#DKW6764, mat_jan_3. wet, dry mass, bulk nr correct. REDY AND WEIGH. still outlier, keep in.

# checking includes seeing that weight on datasheet and on envelope is the same, that bulk nr is right at both wet and dry mass, reweighing to get new dry mass value in case that is wrong. 

#richard says to keep the remaining ones in dataset, as they are not on the trendline, so biologiacally it makes sense. all this means is that DLW1760 is big leaves. And DKW6764 is small.
#add comment on this 

# Use mutate() and str_c() to add comments to a string
data <- data |> 
  mutate(remark = ifelse(
    envelopeID %in% c("DLW1760"),
    str_c(coalesce(remark, ""), "; outlier, big leaf"),
    remark
  ))

data <- data |> 
  mutate(remark = ifelse(
    envelopeID %in% c("DKW6764"),
    str_c(coalesce(remark, ""), "; outlier, small leaf"),
    remark
  ))

# DMW4615 has very high LDMC value with 0.99. After quality control cannot figure out where the mistake is or correct it. 
# Therefore, REMOVING ROW IN CODE HERE: 

data <- data |>  
  filter(envelopeID != "DMW4615" | is.na(envelopeID))



# ALTHOUGH NOT AS EXTREME, HAVE GONE THROUGH THE OTHER OTLIERS IN THE SAME MANNER AND CROSS CHECKED WITH ENVELOPES. ENTERED VALUES FOR WET AND DRY MASS ARE MATHCING ENVELOPES.

```

#### Rerun dry mass / wet mass after removing impossible value

```{r}
#| label: rerun dry vs wet mass
#| message: false
ggplot(data |> filter(proj_2025 == "Traits"),
       aes(x = wet_mass_mean, y = dry_mass_mean)) +
  
  # scatter points
  geom_point(aes(colour = factor(plant_nr)),
             size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
  
  # 1:1 reference line
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0,
              linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
    geom_abline(slope = .5, intercept = 0,
              linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50")+
  
  # fitted regression line
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "blue") +
  facet_wrap(vars(month)) +
  # optional: label
  labs(
    x = "Wet mass per leaf (g)",
    y = "Dry mass per leaf (g)",
    title = "Dry mass vs wet mass per leaf",
    subtitle = "Dashed line = 1:1 reference, blue line = fitted linear trend",
    colour = "plant_nr"
  ) +
  
  theme_bw()

# problematic point removed successfully
```

### Q-Q plot and skewness LDMC

```{r}
#| label: Q-Q and skewness LDMC
#| message: false

qq_trait_var_ldmc <- "ldmc"

# filtering for Traits dataset and calculating raw + log1p versions
df_compare_ldmc <- data |> 
  filter(proj_2025 == "Traits") |> 
  filter(!is.na(.data[[qq_trait_var_ldmc]])) |> 
  mutate(
    raw = .data[[qq_trait_var_ldmc]],
    log1p = log1p(raw)
  ) |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(raw, log1p),
    names_to = "version",
    values_to = "value"
  )

# creating  Q-Q plot
ggplot(df_compare_ldmc, aes(sample = value)) +
  stat_qq(alpha = 0.35) +
  stat_qq_line() +
  facet_grid(version + age_class ~ month, scales = "free") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = paste("QQ plots (raw vs log1p) for", qq_trait_var_ldmc),
    subtitle = "Straighter points means closer to normality within each Month x age_class",
    x = "Theoretical quantiles",
    y = "Sample quantiles"
  )

# SKEWNESS
# calculating skew proxy 
skew_table_ldmc <- df_compare_ldmc |> 
  group_by(month, age_class, version) |> 
  summarise(
    n = sum(!is.na(value)),
    mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    skew_proxy = (mean - median) / sd,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

skew_table_ldmc

# plotting skewness
ggplot(skew_table_ldmc,
       aes(x = month,
           y = skew_proxy,
           colour = version)) +
  
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  
  geom_point(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.4),
    size = 3
  ) +
  
  facet_grid(~ age_class) +
  
  theme_bw() +
  
  labs(
    title = paste("Skewness comparison (raw vs log1p) for", qq_trait_var_ldmc),
    y = "Skew Proxy: (mean - median) / sd",
    colour = "Version"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: SLA vs LDMC
#| message: false

# logically these should be negatively correlated, high SLA would mean low LDMC (og motsatt)

ggplot(
  data,
  aes(x = ldmc, y = sla)
) +
  geom_point(aes(colour = age_class), alpha = 0.6) +
  facet_grid(age_class ~ month) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "LDMC",
    y = "SLA (cmB2/g)",
    title = "Trait consistency check: SLA vs LDMC"
  ) 
  
```

### Combining all remarks in remark column

```{r}
#| label: remarks combined 
#| message: false

# ugly code, had to force using dplyr, not sure why
# stitching all comments together in the remark column, given that remarks start with ; and are in a column starting with remark_

data <- data |> 
  dplyr::rowwise() |> 
  dplyr::mutate(remark = paste(
    c(remark, dplyr::c_across(dplyr::starts_with("remark_"))),
    collapse = ""
  )) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(remark = gsub("NA", "", remark)) |> 
  dplyr::ungroup()

# removing excess remark columns as they are now duplicate 
data <- data |> 
  dplyr::select(-dplyr::starts_with("remark_"))

```

### Save and export clean data

```{r}
#| label: save clean data
#| message: false

# saving clean data in a csv
# data is already on long format, no need to pivot longer

# Ensure that only `NA` values are represented as "NA" in the CSV. Using relative path, 'here' after changing folder structure 
write.csv(
  data, here("clean_data", "DURIN_Clean_Seasonal_Thermal_Tolerance_Bente_Msc.csv")
)

```
